candidate: Daria Wrubel — Berkeley City Council D1
org_research: United Auto Workers Region 6 represents workers across Northern California and endorses candidates in municipal races. Their questionnaire probes labor solidarity, union affiliation, and willingness to actively support organizing drives — including attending picket lines and writing support letters. It also covers housing, climate, campaign finance, and endorsements, reflecting UAW's broad interest in worker-adjacent policy.

contact_fields.label: Union Affiliation, if any (both past and current)

questions.question: Why should the UAW support your candidacy?
If elected (or re-elected), what would be your top three issues?
Are you affiliated with a political party?
What role does the office you seek play in labor relations?
Have you, or any immediate family member, ever belonged to a union?  If so, describe any union activities.

If workers in your city were involved in a union organizing drive, would you be willing to speak out on behalf of workers' rights to organize?  Would you be willing to write a letter of support? Would you attend a rally? A picket line?
How will you improve access to affordable housing in urban areas? What new policies or strengthened enforcements can address racial discrimination in housing and stop the epidemic of evictions?
Please cite policy measures around transit, emission reduction, infrastructure and other related policy areas that you would support in relation to mitigating climate change.
How are you financing your campaign? Are you accepting/seeking support of corporations or corporate-sponsored PACs?
Who has endorsed your campaign? Please name labor unions, political organizations, elected officials, and community leaders.
